title: Effect of Stretching (FNS) and Strengthening Exercise (WBE) for Diabetic Neuropathy
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8poj9","text":"Type 2 diabetes mellitus is increasing globally and diabetic neuropathy substantially impairs quality of life and functional mobility. Effective non-invasive treatments remain limited, particularly in resource-constrained settings. This randomized controlled trial will evaluate the effectiveness of functional neural stretching and weight-bearing exercise versus standard care in individuals with diabetic neuropathy. A total of 84 participants aged 40–80 years with type 2 diabetes and diabetic neuropathy of at least 1 year’s duration will be recruited from three centers in Dhaka and Jashore and randomly allocated to experimental or control groups (n=42 each). The intervention will last 8 weeks, with assessments at baseline, post-intervention, and 24-week follow-up, measuring pain intensity and neuropathy symptoms as primary outcomes, and ankle range of motion, functional mobility, muscle strength, and quality of life as secondary outcomes. The trial has received ethical approval from the Institutional Review Board of the Department of Physiotherapy \u0026 Rehabilitation, Jashore University of Science \u0026 Technology (PTR-JUST/IRB/2025/04/08).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"ck7as","text":"During treatment, exercises must be performed within a pain-free range with careful monitoring of blood glucose, fatigue, and balance. Therapy should be stopped immediately if the patient develops dizziness, excessive pain, or hypoglycemic symptoms. Extra caution is required for foot protection to prevent injury due to reduced sensation. Avoid overexertion by progressing gradually, and ensure all sessions are supervised with prompt adjustment if any adverse effects occur.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"8hv1r","text":"Primary Outcome Tools:","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fc3i","text":"• Pain: Visual Analog Scale (VAS)","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1i69a","text":"• Neuropathy Symptoms: Michigan Neuropathy Screening Instrument (MNSI)","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cd40d","text":"Secondary Outcome Tools:","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":24}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b3663","text":"• Ankle ROM: Goniometer","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d8deu","text":"• Mobility: Timed Up and Go (TUG) Test","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"r1j7","text":"• Strength: Manual Muscle Testing (MMT), ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a667b","text":"WHOQOL: ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9tn2j","text":"• World Health Organization Quality of Life assessment.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
